topic: NLP4PBM: A Systematic Review on Process Extraction using Natural Language Processing with Rule-based, Machine and Deep Learning Methods
topic_definition: Automated process extraction is the task of transforming natural-language descriptions of activities, actors, and control flow into structured process representations or process models using natural language processing.

summary_topics.description: NLP4PBM: A Systematic Review on Process Extraction using Natural Language Processing with Rule-based, Machine and Deep Learning Methods

required.criterion: Full research article / full research paper.
Primary research (original contribution; not secondary research).
Specifically covers use of NLP for process extraction from natural language text.
Describes experiments to empirically validate the method.

exclusion_criteria.criterion: Not a full research paper, such as a poster, short paper, abstract, report, thesis, or book chapter.

Not specifically NLP-for-process-extraction, including process redesign, matching, prediction, sentiment analysis, targeting individual labels instead of natural text, or generating natural text from processes.
Only superficially discusses an applied method.
Does not describe experiments to validate the method.
